title: Ranked questions
description: ranking — the ranked-choice type. The answer is an ORDERED array of option values; the array position is the rank (first = rank 1). fact_response_item fans it out to one row per ranked option, each carrying its own option_key AND a rank column. Compare to multi_select.json (checkbox): both fan out to option_key rows, but ranking additionally populates rank, and the order is meaningful.

elements.name: priorities
elements.title: Rank these from most to least important to you
elements.description: Validated exactly like a single-select: a non-empty, duplicate-free `choices` list. Downstream, an ordered answer like ["quality", "speed", "cost"] becomes three fact rows — quality rank 1, speed rank 2, cost rank 3 — one per option_key.

elements.name: channels
elements.title: Rank your preferred contact channels
elements.description: Object choices carry a stable machine `value` plus a human `text`, just like single-select; dim_option keys on `value` and rank comes from the submitted array order.
